title: Double immunostaining for PIP2 and phosphorylated ERK
author: Hiraku Miyagi
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.9gxh3xn

steps.instruction: Cultivation of cells on cover slips See "Materials" to sterilize cover slips. Wash cells in a 60-mm dish with 3 mL of DPBS. Add 300 µL of Accumax (Funakoshi) to the dish. Incubate 10 min at 37° C in an incubator with 5% CO 2 . Suspend cells in 0.9 mL of DPBS containing 0.5 mM EDTA. Transfer this suspension to a 1.5 mL tube and count the number of cells using a hemocytometer. Dilute the cells to 2.5 x 10 4 cells/mL in DMEM. Place a sterilized cover slip in each well of a covered 24-well plate as needed. Then transfer the diluted cell suspension to each of those wells (400 µL/well). Leave the plate on the bench for 10 min. Place the plate in an incubator at 37° C with 5% CO 2 and cultivate for 12 hr. In order to starve the cells, replace the medium with 600 µL/well of culture medium without serum.

Activation of cells with EGF Remove 300 µL of medium from each well, leaving 300 µL/well. Add 100 µL of EGF solution (400 ng/mL in DMEM, final concentration is 100 ng/mL) or add medium without ligand. Incubate for 5 min at RT.

First fixation and permeabilization Replace the medium with 400 µL of 3% PFA/NaF/Na 3 VO 4. Incubate for 10 min at RT. Rinse x1 and Wash x3 with HBSS (see Materials) Replace HBSS with 400 µL of 50 ug/mL digitonin/3% PFA/NaF/Na 3 VO 4. Incubate for 10 min at RT. Rinse x1 and Wash x3 with HBSS.
First blocking Remove the buffer solution from the 24-well dish. Block cells for 30 min at RT with 300 µL of Blocking One-P (Nacalai).

Labeling with anti-PIP2 antibody Transfer cover slips from the 24-well plate to the humidified box, placing them on the parafilm. Excess solution on cover slips must be blotted before placing them on the parafilm. Put 40 µL of anti-PIP 2 antibody solution in blocking One-P (1:600) on each cover slip. Incubate for 30 min at RT. Remove the antibody solution from cover slips in the humidified box and then immediately transfer them to wells of a covered 24-well plate to prevent drying. Rinse x1 and Wash x3 with HBSS.

Second fixation and permeabilization Replace the medium with 400 µL of 3% PFA/NaF/Na 3 VO 4. Incubate for 10 min at RT. Rinse x1 and Wash x3 with HBSS (see Materials). Replace HBSS with 400 µL of 0.01% Triton X-100/3% PFA/NaF/Na 3 VO 4. Incubate for 10 min at RT. Rinse x1 and Wash x3 with HBSS.
Second blocking Discard buffer from the 24-well dish. Block cells for 30 min at RT with 300 µL of Blocking One-P (Nacalai).

Labeling with TRITC-conjugated antibody Transfer cover slips from the 24-well plate to the humidified box. Excess solution on cover slips must be blotted before placing on the parafilm. Put 40 µL of TRITC-conjugated anti-mouse IgM antibody solution (1:2400) in Blocking One-P on each cover slip. Incubate for 30 min at RT. Remove the antibody solution from each cover slip in the humidified box and then immediately transfer them to wells of the covered 24-well plate to prevent drying. Rinse x1 and Wash x3 with HBSS.

Labeling with fluorescent dye-conjugated ppERK antibodies Transfer cover slips from the 24-well plate to the humified box. Excess solution on cover slips must be blotted before placing them on the parafilm. Put 40 µL of Alexa647-conjugated anti-ppERK solution (1:300) on each cover slip. Incubate for 30 min at RT. Remove the antibody solution from the cover slips and then immediately transfer them to a covered 24-well plate to prevent drying. Rinse x1 and Wash x3 with HBSS.
Mounting Mount cover slips on glass slides with 4 uL of Abberior mount solid. Store at 4° C in the dark overnight.

Observation and measurement of intensity Fluorescent images of cells are captured using a fluorescence microscope. We use an Olympus IX-81 with an sCMOS camera, ORCA Flash4.0 (Hamamatsu) and a 20x objective lens, UPLSAPO 20x (Olympus). The microscope is controlled using Metamolph (Molecular Devices). Filter sets used to capture fluorescent signals are as follows: U-MRFPHQ (Olympus) for TRITC U-M41008HQ (Olympus) for Alexa647 Fluorescence intensity is measured using ImageJ software (NIH). An example of a fluorescent image is shown in Fig. 1 and distribution of cellular fluorescence intensities is shown in Fig. 2. Figure 1. HeLa cells were stimulated with EGF and then labeled with anti-PIP 2 antibody and anti-ppERK antibody as described above. Figure 2. Fluorescence intensities of anti-PIP 2 antibody-labeled cells decreased significantly (p = 3.43 x 10 -8 ) after ligand treatment. In contrast, the ppERK signal was significantly increased (p = 1.67 x 10 -61 ) after ligand treatment.
